title: Pessopteris nidularis
taxonomy: Pessopteris nidularis is classified as a species of extinct fern-like plant that belonged to the family Dipteridaceae. It is an extinct genus of plants, which were part of the ancient flora of the Paleogene and Eocene periods. The classification of Pessopteris nidularis is a subject of ongoing research in paleobotany. The plant is known for its unique leaf morphology and is often found in fossil records from Europe and North America. Pessopteris nidularis is considered a key fossil species for understanding the evolution of ancient plant life.
preparations: Pessopteris nidularis is a type of extinct conifer, but it doesn't seem to be directly associated with herbal preparations that are used in the modern sense.
